provenance_model_note: This document describes report_subject_commit. It cannot embed its own containing commit SHA because the SHA is unknown until after the commit is created. Resolve the containing commit externally with git log -1 --format=%H -- <file-path>.
evidence_sources: native ACL comparison harness on disposable directories

nfd_001_remediation.description: consume_event() expiry check used real wall clock; tests used a hardcoded frozen clock. CI passed before 12:30 UTC; local audit at 12:40 UTC triggered a spurious REJECT.
nfd_001_remediation.fix: now_utc parameter added to consume_event() and _is_expired(). None uses real wall clock (production). Timezone-aware datetime is normalised to UTC. Naive datetime raises EventConsumerError. 21 deterministic boundary tests added in engine/tests/unit/test_event_consumer_clock.py.

ppa_0001_remediation.description: Provenance model was self-referential: observed_remote_head, implementation_commit, status_report_commit, remediation_commit all attempted to track the latest commit SHA inside files contained in that same commit.
ppa_0001_remediation.fix: Replaced with non-self-referential model: report_subject_commit (what this document describes), validated_subject_commit (what has CI workflow evidence), containing_commit_resolution (how to find the containing commit after push), provenance_model_version. provenance_model_version = non-self-referential-v1.

notes: Latest verified browser helper failure occurs before user JavaScript executes.
Native Windows ACL tests passed on the disposable path classes used for comparison.
Full soak remains not ready because the browser E2E gate is still blocked upstream.
This document describes 938668f1cb14492f0d3236230e68513977c1faf3. Its own containing commit SHA must be resolved externally after push.
